title: Removable dental appliance including spring bellows
abstract: A removable dental appliance may include an appliance body at least partially surrounding two or more teeth of a patient. The appliance body may include a first shell shaped to receive a first tooth; a second shell shaped to receive a second tooth; and at least one spring bellows that includes an arcuate displacement extending over and away from at least a portion of an interproximal region between the first tooth and the second tooth to join the first shell and the second shell. The spring bellows is configured to apply a force between the first shell and the second shell to cause movement of at least one of the first tooth and the second tooth toward a desired position when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A removable dental appliance comprising: an appliance body configured to at least partially surround two or more teeth of a patient, wherein the appliance body comprises: a first shell shaped to receive a first tooth of the patient; a second shell shaped to receive a second tooth of the patient; and at least one spring bellows comprising an arcuate displacement of the appliance body extending over and away from at least a portion of an interproximal region between the first tooth and the second tooth to join the first shell and the second shell, wherein the at least one spring bellows is configured to apply a force between the first shell and the second shell to cause movement of at least one of the first tooth and the second tooth toward a desired position when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 2. The removable dental appliance of claim 1, wherein the removable dental appliance comprises an aligning tray. 3. The removable dental appliance of claim 1 or 2, wherein the at least one spring bellows overlaps at least a portion of the gingival margin. 4. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein a thickness of the at least one spring bellows is less than a thickness of the first and second shells to at least one of concentrate strain in the at least one spring bellows or reduce deformation of the first shell and the second shell when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 5. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein a thickness of the at least one spring bellows is between about 0.025 millimeters and about 1.0 millimeter, and the thickness of the first and second shells is between about 0.25 millimeters and about 2.0 millimeters. 6. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the thickness of the at least one spring bellows is constant along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 7. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the thickness of the at least one spring bellows varies along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 8. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the arcuate displacement comprises an outer radius of curvature between about 0.5 millimeters and about 2 millimeters. 9. The removable dental appliance of claim 8, wherein the outer radius of curvature is substantially constant along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 10. The removable dental appliance of claim 8, wherein the outer radius of curvature varies along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 11. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein at least a portion of an edge of the at least one spring bellows joins at least a portion of an edge of the first shell at a first intersection. 12. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein at least a portion of an edge of the at least one spring bellows joins at least a portion of the second shell at a second intersection displaced by a distance from an edge of the second shell. 13. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein at least one spring bellows defines a shear reduction region. 14. The removable dental appliance of claim 13, wherein the shear reduction region comprises a single cutout in the at least one spring bellows. 15. The removable dental appliance of claim 13, wherein the shear reduction region comprises a plurality of cutouts in the at least one spring bellows. 16. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the at least one spring bellows extends around at least a portion of at least one of a cervical margin or a gingival margin of at least one of the first tooth or the second tooth. 17. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 1 to 16, wherein the appliance body comprises a unitary three-dimensionally printed polymeric material. 18. A system comprising: an ordered set of removable dental appliances configured to reposition one or more teeth of a patient, each removable dental appliance in the set of removable dental appliances comprising a respective appliance body configured to at least partially surround two or more teeth of the patient, wherein each respective appliance body comprises: a first shell shaped to receive a first tooth of the patient; a second shell shaped to receive a second tooth of the patient; and at least one spring bellows comprising an arcuate displacement of the appliance body extending over and away from at least a portion of an interproximal region between the first tooth and the second tooth to join the first shell and the second shell, wherein the at least one spring bellows is configured to apply a force between the first shell and the second shell to cause movement of at least one of the first tooth and the second tooth toward a desired position when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 19. The system of claim 18, wherein each removable dental appliance in the set of removable dental appliances comprises an aligning tray. 20. The system of claim 18 or 19, wherein the at least one spring bellows overlaps at least a portion of the gingival margin. 21. The system of any one of claims 18 to 20, wherein a thickness of the at least one spring bellows is less than a thickness of the first and second shells to at least one of concentrate strain in the at least one spring bellows or reduce deformation of the first shell and the second shell when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 22. The system of any one of claims 18 to 21, wherein a thickness of the at least one spring bellows is between about 0.025 millimeters and about 1.0 millimeter, and the thickness of the first and second shells is between about 0.25 millimeters and about 2.0 millimeters. 23. The system of any one of claims 18 to 22, wherein the thickness of the at least one spring bellows is constant along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 24. The system of any one of claims 18 to 22, wherein the thickness of the at least one spring bellows varies along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 25. The system of any one of claims 18 to 24, wherein the arcuate displacement comprises an outer radius of curvature between about 0.5 millimeters and about 2 millimeters. 26. The system of claim 25, wherein the outer radius of curvature is substantially constant along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 27. The system of claim 25, wherein the outer radius of curvature varies along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 28. The system of any one of claims 18 to 27, wherein at least a portion of an edge of the at least one spring bellows joins at least a portion of an edge of the first shell at a first intersection. 29. The system of any one of claims 18 to 28, wherein at least a portion of an edge of the at least one spring bellows joins at least a portion of the second shell at a second intersection displaced by a distance from an edge of the second shell. 30. The system of any one of claims 18 to 29, wherein at least one spring bellows defines a shear reduction region. 31. The system of claim 30, wherein the shear reduction region comprises a single cutout in the at least one spring bellows. 32. The system of claim 30, wherein the shear reduction region comprises a plurality of cutouts in the at least one spring bellows. 33. The system of any one of claims 18 to 32, wherein the at least one spring bellows extends around at least one of a cervical margin or a gingival margin of at least one of the first tooth or the second tooth. 34. The system of any one of claims 18 to 33, wherein the appliance body comprises a unitary three-dimensionally printed polymeric material. 35. A method comprising: forming a model of dental anatomy of a patient; and forming, based on the model, a removable dental appliance comprising an appliance body configured to at least partially surround two or more teeth of the patient, wherein the appliance body comprises: a first shell shaped to receive a first tooth of the patient; a second shell shaped to receive a second tooth of the patient; and at least one spring bellows comprising an arcuate displacement of the appliance body extending over and away from at least a portion of an interproximal region between the first tooth and the second tooth to join the first shell and the second shell, wherein the at least one spring bellows is configured to apply a force between the first shell and the second shell to cause movement of at least one of the first tooth and the second tooth toward a desired position when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the removable dental appliance comprises an aligning tray. 37. The method of claim 35 or 36, wherein the at least one spring bellows overlaps at least a portion of the gingival margin. 38. The method of any one of claims 35 to 37, wherein a thickness of the at least one spring bellows is less than a thickness of the first and second shells to at least one of concentrate strain in the at least one spring bellows or reduce deformation of the first shell and the second shell when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 39. The method of any one of claims 35 to 38, wherein a thickness of the at least one spring bellows is between about 0.025 millimeters and about 1.0 millimeter, and the thickness of the first and second shells is between about 0.25 millimeters and about 2.0 millimeters. 40. The method of any one of claims 35 to 39, wherein the thickness of the at least one spring bellows is constant along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 41. The method of any one of claims 35 to 40, wherein the thickness of the at least one spring bellows varies along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 42. The method of any one of claims 35 to 41, wherein the arcuate displacement comprises an outer radius of curvature between about 0.5 millimeters and about 2 millimeters. 43. The method of claim 42, wherein the outer radius of curvature is substantially constant along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 44. The method of claim 42, wherein the outer radius of curvature varies along an interproximal boundary curve extending from the lingual gingiva along the interproximal region to the facial gingiva. 45. The method of any one of claims 35 to 44, at least a portion of an edge of the at least one spring bellows joins at least a portion of an edge the first shell at a first intersection. 46. The method of any one of claims 35 to 45, wherein at least a portion of an edge of the at least one spring bellows joins at least a portion of the second shell at a second intersection displaced by a distance from an edge of the second shell. 47. The method of any one of claims 35 to 46, wherein at least one spring bellows defines a shear reduction region. 48. The method of claim 47, wherein the shear reduction region comprises a single cutout in the at least one spring bellows. 49. The method of claim 47, wherein the shear reduction region comprises a plurality of cutouts in the at least one spring bellows. 50. The method of any one of claims 35 to 49, wherein the at least one spring bellows extends around at least one of a cervical margin or a gingival margin of at least one of the first tooth or the second tooth. 51. The method of any one of claims 35 to 50, wherein the appliance body comprises a unitary three-dimensionally printed polymeric material. 52. A method comprising: receiving, by a computing device, a digital representation of a three-dimensional (3D) dental anatomy of a patient, the dental anatomy providing initial positions of one or more teeth of the patient; determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of a removable dental appliance for the patient, wherein: the removable dental appliance comprises an appliance body configured to at least partially surround two or more teeth of the patient, the dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance are configured to reposition the one or more teeth of the patient from initial positions to desired positions when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient, and the dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance comprise: a position, dimension and shape of a first shell shaped to receive a first tooth of the patient; a position, dimension and shape of a second shell shaped to receive a second tooth of the patient; a position, dimension, and shape of at least one spring bellows comprising an arcuate displacement of the appliance body extending over and away from at least a portion of an interproximal region between the first tooth and the second tooth to join the first shell and the second shell, wherein the at least one spring bellows is configured to apply a force between the first shell and the second shell to cause movement of at least one of the first tooth and the second tooth toward a desired position when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient; and transmitting, by the computing device, a representation of the removable dental appliance to a computer-aided manufacturing system. 53. The method of any one of claims 52, wherein the removable dental appliance comprises an aligning tray. 54. The method of claim 52 or 53, wherein the three-dimensional (3D) dental anatomy of the patient further includes at least some portion of tooth roots, gingiva, periodontal ligaments (PDL), alveolar bone, or cortical bone. 55. The method of any one of claims 52 to 54, wherein determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance includes accepting input from a user, wherein the input influences at least one of the dimensions and shapes. 56. The method of any one of claims 52 to 55, wherein determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance includes automatically determining at least one of the dimensions and shapes. 57. The method of any one of claims 52 to 56, wherein determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance includes presenting a representation of the removable dental appliance to a user for review. 58. The method of any one of claims 52 to 57, wherein transmitting the representation of the removable dental appliance includes sending a digital model of the removable dental appliance from the computing device to the computer-aided manufacturing system, and manufacturing at least a portion of the removable dental appliance with the computer-aided manufacturing system according to the digital model from the computing device. 59. The method of claim 58, wherein the computer-aided manufacturing system comprises a 3D printer, and at least some portion of the removable dental appliance is formed using the 3D printer. 60. The method of any one of claims 52 to 59, further comprising: determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of each of an ordered set of a removable dental appliances for the patient, the removable dental appliance being one of the ordered set of removable dental appliances for the patient, 

 wherein each removable dental appliance in the ordered set of removable dental appliances is configured to incrementally reposition the teeth of the patient to a more advanced position than any one of the earlier removable dental appliances within the set of the removable dental appliances. 61. The method of any one of claims 52 to 60, wherein determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance includes selecting, by the computing device, the dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance according to a set of predefined design constraints, the set of predefined design constraints including one or more of a group consisting of: a minimum and a maximum localized force applied to one or more of the surrounded teeth, the first shell, or the second shell; a minimum and a maximum rotational force applied to one or more of the surrounded teeth, the first shell, or the second shell; a minimum and a maximum translational force applied to one or more of the surrounded teeth, the first shell, or the second shell; a minimum and a maximum total force applied to one or more of the surrounded teeth, the first shell, or the second shell; and a minimum and a maximum strain applied to the removable dental appliance when worn by the patient. 62. The method of any one of claims 52 to 61, wherein determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance includes modifying the initial positions of one or more teeth of the patient to produce a modified dental anatomy, wherein the modified dental anatomy represents an incremental repositioning of the one or more teeth of the patient as compared to the initial positions of the one or more teeth of the patient, and the dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance conform to the modified dental anatomy. 63. The method of any one of claims 52 to 62, wherein determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance further comprise a position, dimension, and shape of a shear reduction region. 64. The method of claim 63, wherein the shear reduction region comprises at least one cutout in the at least one spring bellows. 65. The method of any one of claims 52 to 64, wherein determining, by the computing device, dimensions and shapes of the removable dental appliance includes selecting a thickness of the at least one spring bellows to provide a stiffness suitable to reposition the one or more teeth of the patient from their initial positions to adjusted positions when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 66. The method of any one of claims 52 to 65, wherein the at least one spring bellows overlaps at least a portion of the gingival margin. 67. The method of any one of claims 52 to 66, wherein: a thickness of the at least one spring bellows is less than a thickness of the first and second shells to at least one of concentrate strain in the at least one spring bellows or reduce deformation of the first shell and the second shell when the removable dental appliance is worn by the patient. 68. The removable dental appliance of any one of claims 52 to 67, wherein the at least one spring bellows extends around at least one of a cervical margin or a gingival margin of at least one of the first tooth or the second tooth. 69. The method of any one of claims 52 to 68, wherein the appliance body comprises a unitary three-dimensionally printed polymeric material. 70. The method of any one of claims 52 to 69, wherein the computing device includes a plurality of computing devices operably connected via one or more computer networks. 71. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium that stores computer system- executable instructions that, when executed, configure a processor to perform the method of any of claims 52 to 70.
